entryA.symptom: Linux VM fails to boot after migration to Azure or after disabling Hyper-V drivers. VM stuck at dracut/initramfs, kernel panic, cannot find root device, no network, GRUB failure. Missing hv_vmbus/hv_s
entryA.solution: Fix1(disabled): chroot repair VM, grep -nr hv_ /etc/modprobe.d/, remove blacklist. Fix2(missing): add hv_vmbus hv_netvsc hv_storvsc to /etc/dracut.conf or /etc/initramfs-tools/modules, rebuild initramfs.

entryB.symptom: Windows Server VM fails to boot: winload.exe not found or BCD boot failure; VM stuck at boot error screen after disk corruption or migration
entryB.solution: 1) Attach failed OS disk to troubleshooting VM; identify drive letters (System Reserved active partition = BCD location, e.g. H:; OS partition = I:); 2) bcdedit /store H:\boot\bcd /enum to view entries; 3) Fix {default}: set device partition=I:, osdevice partition=I:, integrityservices enable, recov

conflicts.reasoning: False alarm: entryA is Linux Hyper-V driver boot fix, entryB is Windows BCD boot fix - completely different OS and scenarios. Keyword "disable/enable" is coincidental.

entryA.symptom: Mount of Azure File Share on RHEL 8.2 fails with: mount: bad option; for several filesystems (e.g. nfs, cifs) you might need a /sbin/mount.<type> helper program
entryA.solution: Install cifs-utils package: yum install cifs-utils. Verify installation: ls -l /sbin/mount.cifs. Then retry mounting the Azure File Share. The package provides mount.cifs binary required for CIFS/SMB mounts.

entryB.symptom: apt update fails with does not have a Release file for armhf packages on x86_64 Azure VM
entryB.solution: Remove or comment out armhf repository lines from sources.list or sources.list.d/*.list.

conflicts.reasoning: False alarm: entryA is CIFS mount on RHEL (install cifs-utils), entryB is apt armhf repo on Debian (remove wrong repo). Different packages, different distros, different issues.

entryA.symptom: VM extension fails with Error Code 51 'Unsupported OS'. Extension cannot run on the current operating system.
entryA.rootCause: The VM's operating system is not supported by the specific extension. Windows VM Agent requires Windows Server 2008 SP2 (64-bit) + .NET 4.0 minimum. Linux agent has extension framework limits beyond base agent support.

entryB.symptom: Set-AzVMCustomScriptExtension fails on Linux VM with error: binCustomScriptHandler.exe command not found; exit code 127; extension handler reports terminal failure for Microsoft.Compute.CustomScriptEx
entryB.rootCause: Set-AzVMCustomScriptExtension installs from publisher Microsoft.Compute.CustomScriptExtension which is only supported for Windows; Linux requires publisher Microsoft.Azure.Extensions

conflicts.reasoning: False alarm: entryA is generic extension OS support matrix, entryB is CSE publisher mismatch (Windows vs Linux). Different root issues, keyword "supported/not supported" is coincidental.
